description: Arc flash warning labels missing from electrical panels, switchgear, or motor control centers where required by NFPA 70E
visual_signals: electrical panel front with no arc flash hazard label visible
switchgear door lacking orange arc flash warning sticker
motor control center bucket without arc flash PPE requirement label

description: Electrical panels or switchgear lacking required 36-inch clear working space in front per NEC 110.26
visual_signals: boxes or storage items stacked directly in front of electrical panel
equipment positioned within 36-inch clearance zone of breaker panel
shelving unit encroaching on required working space before switchgear

description: Open or missing knockout covers on electrical panels, junction boxes, or enclosures exposing interior wiring
visual_signals: circular hole visible in side or bottom of electrical panel where knockout is missing
junction box with open knockout hole showing wiring inside
conduit fitting missing from panel knockout leaving open gap

description: Signs of electrical overheating including discoloration, melted insulation, scorch marks, or heat damage on panels or conductors
visual_signals: brown or black heat discoloration on panel bus connection or breaker
melted wire insulation with exposed conductor visible inside panel
scorch marks or charring on panel interior wall or breaker face

description: Panel dead-front cover removed or missing, exposing energized bus bars and creating electrocution and arc flash hazard
visual_signals: electrical panel open with bus bars and wiring fully exposed
missing inner dead-front cover with bare copper bus visible
panel cover off with energized connections accessible to contact

description: Extension cords used as substitute for permanent wiring, routed through walls, ceilings, or floors, or daisy-chained in series
visual_signals: extension cord routed through doorway or wall penetration as permanent installation
extension cord taped to baseboard or stapled along wall surface
multiple extension cords plugged end-to-end in daisy chain configuration

description: Water intrusion, moisture, or condensation present on or near energized electrical equipment creating shock and short-circuit hazard
visual_signals: water puddle on floor directly beneath electrical panel or disconnect
condensation droplets visible on exterior of electrical enclosure
water stain trail running down wall from above electrical panel

description: Circuit breakers and panelboard directory not labeled or directory incomplete, preventing proper circuit identification per NEC 408.4
visual_signals: panelboard with blank circuit directory card inside door
breaker panel with handwritten labels that are faded and illegible
panel directory with most circuit entries left blank or marked unknown

description: Cable trays overfilled beyond rated capacity or cables unsupported between tray sections creating sag and stress on connections
visual_signals: cable tray with cables piled above side rail height
cables sagging between cable tray support points
cable tray section visibly deflecting under excessive cable weight

description: Conduit damaged, disconnected, or missing sections leaving conductors exposed and unprotected from physical damage
visual_signals: broken or crushed conduit section with wires visible through opening
conduit disconnected at fitting with gap exposing conductors
missing conduit section with bare wires running along wall surface

description: Emergency electrical disconnects blocked, obstructed, or not readily accessible for immediate operation in an emergency
visual_signals: equipment or storage blocking access to red emergency disconnect handle
emergency shutoff switch obscured behind parked equipment
disconnect switch handle inaccessible due to items piled in front

description: Combustible materials including cardboard, paper, wood, or flammable liquids stored inside dedicated electrical rooms violating NEC 110.26(E)
visual_signals: cardboard boxes stored on floor of electrical room
paper files or documents stacked on shelf inside electrical room
cleaning supplies or chemical containers stored in electrical room

description: Arc flash PPE boundary markings missing from floor in front of switchgear or large electrical equipment per NFPA 70E
visual_signals: floor in front of switchgear lineup without any arc flash boundary tape or paint
faded or worn arc flash boundary marking on floor before electrical equipment
switchgear room floor with no visible limited approach or arc flash boundary lines

description: Switchgear room used for storage of non-electrical items, containing piping not serving the room, or housing unrelated equipment violating dedicated space requirements
visual_signals: janitorial supplies or cleaning equipment stored inside switchgear room
water pipes or plumbing running through switchgear room ceiling not serving equipment
furniture or filing cabinets placed inside dedicated electrical switchgear room

description: GFCI protection missing at receptacles in wet or damp locations including near sinks, outdoors, garages, and unfinished basements per NEC 210.8
visual_signals: standard non-GFCI receptacle installed next to sink or wet area
outdoor receptacle without GFCI protection or weather-resistant cover
receptacle near water source without GFCI test and reset buttons visible

description: Transformer showing oil leaks, corrosion, unusual discoloration, or cooling fan not operating indicating degraded condition
visual_signals: oil stain or drip trail on transformer housing or beneath transformer pad
heavy rust or corrosion on transformer tank surface or radiator fins
transformer cooling fan visibly not spinning while unit is under load

description: Grounding electrode conductor showing corrosion, physical damage, or improper connection at grounding electrode or panel
visual_signals: green or bare copper grounding conductor with visible corrosion at clamp connection
grounding electrode conductor with physical cut or abrasion damage to insulation
loose or improperly crimped lug on grounding conductor at panel bonding point

description: Motor control center bucket door damaged, not latching properly, or left open exposing internal starters and wiring
visual_signals: MCC bucket door hanging open with visible starter contactors and wiring
motor control center door with broken latch mechanism not closing flush
MCC bucket with bent or damaged door that does not seat against frame

description: Emergency generator automatic transfer switch showing alarm, set to manual mode, or with expired maintenance tags
visual_signals: transfer switch panel with alarm indicator light illuminated
ATS control selector set to manual or off position instead of auto
expired maintenance or inspection tag hanging from transfer switch handle

description: Evidence of bypassed, double-tapped, or improperly sized overcurrent protection devices creating overload or fire hazard
visual_signals: two conductors landed on single breaker terminal that is rated for one
circuit breaker with visible jumper wire bypassing trip mechanism
fuse holder with oversized fuse or metallic object inserted instead of proper fuse

description: Flexible cord or portable cable showing damage, missing strain relief, or used with improper cord type for the environment
visual_signals: power cord with cracked or frayed outer jacket exposing inner conductors
flexible cord entering junction box or equipment without strain relief fitting
lightweight indoor-rated extension cord being used in outdoor or wet environment

description: Electrical room ambient temperature excessively high or ventilation and cooling equipment non-functional creating thermal risk to equipment
visual_signals: electrical room with no visible ventilation grille or exhaust fan
wall-mounted thermometer in electrical room showing elevated temperature reading
electrical room cooling unit or exhaust fan visibly not operating with dust accumulation

description: Junction box covers missing, exposing wire splices and connections, violating NEC 314.25 requiring all boxes to be covered
visual_signals: open junction box mounted on wall or ceiling with visible wire nuts and splices
junction box in accessible area with cover plate removed showing connections
uncovered octagonal box with exposed wiring at ceiling light fixture location

description: Temporary wiring such as construction lighting strings or seasonal decorations left installed permanently beyond allowable period per NEC 590
visual_signals: temporary construction string lights installed as permanent lighting in work area
holiday or seasonal decorative lights left up well past seasonal period
temporary power distribution panel still in place after construction completion

description: Electrical receptacle showing damage including cracked faceplate, missing ground pin hole, loose mounting, or signs of thermal stress
visual_signals: receptacle faceplate with visible crack or broken corner piece
receptacle pulled away from wall with gap behind cover plate
discoloration or brown heat marks visible on receptacle face or wall around outlet

description: Electrical disconnect switch lacking labeling identifying which specific equipment or circuit it controls per NEC 404.7
visual_signals: disconnect switch on wall with no label or nameplate identifying served equipment
row of disconnect switches without any identifying labels or tags
faded or illegible equipment identification label on disconnect enclosure

description: Panelboard circuit directory inaccurate, outdated, or not reflecting current circuit assignments creating confusion during maintenance or emergency
visual_signals: panelboard directory with crossed-out entries and handwritten corrections
circuit directory with multiple blank entries for occupied breaker positions
panel directory card showing outdated room numbers or equipment no longer present

description: Discoloration at bus bar connections or lug terminations indicating loose connection and resistive heating creating fire risk
visual_signals: brown or rainbow heat discoloration on bus bar at lug connection point
darkened or oxidized conductor insulation at breaker terminal connection
visible heat damage or melting on bus bar splice or tap connection

description: Ground fault indicator tripped or activated on electrical equipment signaling current leakage to ground
visual_signals: ground fault indicator light illuminated on motor or equipment panel
GFCI receptacle with tripped indicator showing red flag
ground fault relay on switchgear showing tripped or alarm status

description: Voltage testing equipment or personal protective equipment absent from area near electrical panels or switchgear where live work may be performed
visual_signals: electrical room with no voltage tester or multimeter visible in designated location
empty PPE storage cabinet or hook near switchgear where gloves should be stored
no visible voltage testing device holder or station near high-voltage equipment

description: Electrical panel or switchgear room door lacking required identification signage or warning labels per NEC 110.22
visual_signals: unmarked door leading to electrical room without danger or high voltage sign
electrical room door lacking required room identification placard
switchgear room entrance without visible electrical hazard warning signage

description: Wiring devices or equipment installed without proper weatherproof covers in outdoor or weather-exposed locations per NEC 406.9
visual_signals: outdoor receptacle with missing or broken in-use weatherproof cover
exterior junction box without weather-tight cover allowing moisture entry
outdoor disconnect switch with damaged weatherproof enclosure gasket

description: Improper or excessive use of wire nuts, tape splices, or connections outside of approved junction boxes violating NEC splice and junction requirements
visual_signals: wire splice made with electrical tape outside of junction box in open air
wire nut connection visible in open ceiling or wall cavity without box
conductor spliced and taped inside conduit body without proper access

description: Electrical equipment grounding conductor missing, disconnected, or improperly terminated compromising equipment safety ground per NEC 250
visual_signals: green equipment grounding wire cut or disconnected at receptacle box
grounding lug on equipment panel empty where bonding conductor should terminate
missing green grounding screw and conductor on metal device box or enclosure

description: Panelboard or load center with more breakers installed than the panel is rated for, indicating an overfilled or tandem-breaker-exceeded condition
visual_signals: panelboard with tandem or slim breakers filling every position beyond panel rating
panel with breaker extending outside of panel enclosure edge
circuit breakers installed in panel positions not designed for tandem use
